channel.title: CTV News – Queen's Park
channel.description: Queen's Park coverage from CTV News Toronto.

item.title: Ontario will not take over entirety of Toronto Islands: transportation minister
Toronto World Cup seats off FIFA resale site while it retools for price cap law
item.description: FIFA’s marketplace for resale World Cup tickets no longer has seats listed for Toronto matches after a new price cap law came into effect.

item.title: Ontario may ban cellphones outright in schools
item.description: Ontario is considering an outright ban on cellphones in schools, and is looking at restricting social media for kids under a certain age, Ontario Education Minister Paul Calandra says.

item.title: Ontarians with mild hearing loss may soon be able to purchase over-the-counter hearing aids
item.description: Ontarians with mild hearing loss could soon have access to over-the-counter hearing aids, under a regulatory change the government is considering.

item.title: ‘Our members are upset:’ Toronto police union speaks out over Gardiner, DVP changes
item.description: The head of the union representing Toronto police officers says it is both “ineffective and inefficient” to bring in provincial police to patrol the Gardiner Expressway and Don Valley Parkway.

item.title: Ford apologizes after personal insults in legislature, Liberal MPP says
item.description: An Ontario Liberal member of provincial parliament says Premier Doug Ford has apologized to her after he hurled a personal insult at her Thursday in the legislature.

item.title: Ontario hospitals announce job cuts, nearly three-quarters of hospitals in deficit
item.description: Several Ontario hospitals have announced job cuts in recent months as they try to climb out of financial holes, a situation critics say will likely keep repeating.

item.title: Ticketmaster to ban reselling tickets above original price starting next week
item.description: Ticketmaster Canada says fans won’t be able to resell their tickets for more than the original cost starting next week.

item.title: Ontario planning to seize section of waterfront park as part of airport expansion plans
item.description: Ontario Transportation Minister Prabmeet Sarkaria has introduced a bill that would allow the province to take over the City of Toronto’s role in the island airport.

item.title: Ford urged to apologize for personal insults hurled in the legislature
item.description: Ontario’s opposition parties called on Premier Doug Ford to apologize for a personal insult he hurled — and repeated — at a Liberal member of provincial parliament Thursday during a debate about a law that restricts public access to his records.

item.title: Ford government passes budget that lets premier and ministers keep office records secret
item.description: The Ford government has passed a spring budget that includes provisions to let the premier and his cabinet ministers keep all their office records secret.

item.title: ‘This can come to a quick end’: Doug Ford says U.S. economy is losing out as Canadians boycott country
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford says that the U.S. economy is losing out on “tens of billions of dollars” as Canadians continue to boycott the country amid a trade war.

item.title: NDP, Liberals speaking out after Ford government moves to host late-night session amid changes to FOI legislation
item.description: The Ontario NDP and Liberal parties are speaking out after the Ford government moved to host a late-night session on Wednesday, which they both say is being done to push through changes to Freedom of Information (FOI) legislation, effectively blocking access to the premier’s cellphone records.

item.title: Ontario Liberals have first official leadership contender
item.description: The race to become the Ontario Liberals’ next leader has its first official entrant. Dylan Marando, a former political staffer, says on a website launching his bid that he wants to build an Ontario of inclusion, optimism and opportunity.

item.title: Ontario sets financial literacy requirement for high school graduation
item.description: Ontario high school students will soon need to pass a financial literacy test in order to graduate.
